hook.text: Assets normally have debit balances. Revenue normally has credit balances. Each account has a 'home side.'

buildup.text: The normal balance is the side—debit or credit—where increases to that account are recorded.

discovery.text: Assets and expenses are debit-normal. Liabilities, equity, and revenue are credit-normal.

twist.text: A credit balance in an asset account usually signals an error—or an account that's been overdrawn.

climax.text: Knowing normal balances instantly tells you if an account's direction looks right or suspicious.

punchline.text: Every account has a home side. Learn them all.

quiz.question: What is the normal balance of an expense account?
